title: Ansible Roles
learningObjectives: Create and apply roles using ansible-galaxy init
Use role defaults and vars with proper precedence

theory.content:
What is a Role?

A role is a structured way to organize playbook content for reuse and sharing. Roles are the primary mechanism for distributing Ansible automation via Ansible Galaxy.

Role Directory Structure

roles/
  webserver/
    tasks/
      main.yml      # Main task list
    handlers/
      main.yml      # Handlers
    templates/
      nginx.conf.j2 # Jinja2 templates
    files/
      index.html    # Static files
    vars/
      main.yml      # High-priority variables
    defaults/
      main.yml      # Low-priority defaults
    meta/
      main.yml      # Role metadata, dependencies
    README.md

Creating a Role

ansible-galaxy role init webserver

Using Roles in a Playbook

---
- name: Configure web tier
  hosts: webservers
  become: true
  roles:
    - webserver
    - { role: firewall, vars: { open_ports: [80, 443] } }

Role Defaults vs Vars

  • defaults/main.yml — Lowest precedence; safe to override from playbook or inventory
  • vars/main.yml — High precedence; should hold role-internal constants not meant for overriding

Role Dependencies

# meta/main.yml
dependencies:
  - role: common
  - role: nginx
    vars:
      nginx_port: 8080

Official Roles Documentation →
